title: [Alakazam] Battle Guide
summary: A [Psychic] glass-cannon [sweeper] with [magic-guard], 135 SpA, and 120 Speed — the indirect-damage immunity lets [nasty-plot] setups stabilise de[spite] paper-thin bulk.
strengths: Speed 120 outruns most of the top-tier metagame
weaknesses: 55/45/95 bulk is as fragile as it gets
Priority attackers shut down the setup attempt

recommendedMoves.reason: Setup move that doubles SpA in one turn

recommendedItems.reason: Guarantees one setup turn against priority

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Alakazam] is a classic glass-cannon [Psychic] [sweeper] whose identity is defined by the combination of 135 SpA, 120 Speed, and [magic-guard]. The hidden ability negates all indirect damage, which effectively stabilises the paper-thin bulk and enables setup sets that would otherwise be impossible. In Champions, it serves as the dedicated fast special [sweeper] that punishes both offensive and defensive cores.

## Strengths
[magic-guard] is the whole reason this set functions. Every indirect damage source — poison, burn, [stealth-rock], [life-orb] re[coil], [leech-seed] — is negated, letting [Alakazam] live longer than its 55/45/95 bulk suggests. Speed 120 is above most of the upper-tier metagame, and [nasty-plot] doubles SpA into a second-turn sweep win condition. The [psychic] / [focus-blast] / [shadow-ball] combination has no resisted neutral matchups among common walls.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Bulk is the defining vulnerability. 55/45/95 means any neutral priority hit threatens a KO, and the [Dark], [Ghost], and [Bug] 2x weaknesses line up with common cove[rage] moves. Scarfed threats that outspeed unboosted [Alakazam] force a Speed-tier check, and [pursuit]-style trapping (or [shadow-tag] from Mega Gengar) is an effective hard [counter]. Always save [focus-sash] for a priority turn.

## Recommended Moveset
[psychic] is the locked STAB because the 100% accuracy and secondary effect support the sweep. [focus-blast] handles [Dark], [Steel], and [Normal] walls that would resist the [psychic] STAB. [shadow-ball] covers mirror [psychic] matchups and [Ghost] walls that otherwise wall the STAB. The last slot is [nasty-plot], the doubles-SpA setup move that turns a support attacker into a true [sweeper].

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item because it stacks perfectly with the glass-cannon + [magic-guard] identity, guaranteeing one safe setup turn. [life-orb] is the alternative — [magic-guard] nullifies the re[coil], making it a pure 30% damage boost. [choice-scarf] is the Speed-tier reshape that drops setup for [revenge]-killing utility. Ability is [magic-guard], locked. Tera [psychic] maxes STAB while Tera [Fairy] flips [Dark] and [Dragon] weaknesses defensively.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Alakazam] wants [fake-out] leads or pivots that create a safe setup turn, plus [Fairy] or [Steel] partners to cover the [Dark] and [Ghost] weaknesses. [stealth-rock] support pays off without hurting [Alakazam] thanks to [magic-guard]. A priority [block]er — [taunt] lead or a [prankster] ally — shuts down the fast [revenge] killers that threaten the sweep.

## Play Patterns
Enter on a predicted switch, [nasty-plot] on the free turn, then open with [psychic] on neutral matchups, [focus-blast] on [Dark] or [Steel] pivots, and [shadow-ball] on [Ghost] or [psychic] mirrors. Save [focus-sash] for the priority threat, and Tera on any telegraphed [Dark] or [Ghost] move.
